title: ignore_chown_errors config in storage.conf is not respected
body:
skopeo does not seem to respect the `ignore_chown_errors` option while pulling images as container-storage format. Is it supposed to work the same way as podman does? Or is there any other way for skopeo to skip these chown errors.

storage.conf
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
[storage.options.overlay]
ignore_chown_errors = "true"
```

e.g. Assuming there is an image with file owners that are outside the host user's subordinate range. 
```
skopeo copy docker://<image> "containers-storage:[/tmp/docker-test]<image>"
...
FATA[0027] writing blob: adding layer with blob ...: unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 197009:197221 for /example): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run "podman system migrate": lchown /example: invalid argument)
```

Where as the same config with podman pull works correctly

```
podman pull <image>
....
Writing manifest to image destination
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I think the issue is that you are overwriting the graph root and that effectively resets all the other options ignoring completely what you've configured in `storage.conf`.

You need to specify ignore_chown_errors on the CLI as well, or do everything in the `storage.conf` file
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if there is more to the situation.
